title: Intermittent ECONNRESET errors in HTTP Request node
symptom: The HTTP Request node fails intermittently with ECONNRESET errors, indicating that the connection was closed unexpectedly. This occurs across multiple workflows and nodes, roughly once per day.
applies_when: Running on Google Cloud Run with VPC Connector
not_this_if: Error is specific to a single workflow or node
root_cause: In serverless environments like Google Cloud Run, idle TCP connections may be closed by the network. When n8n attempts to reuse these connections, it can result in ECONNRESET errors. This is exacerbated when using a VPC Connector, which may have throughput limits.
fix_steps: Open the HTTP Request node settings.
Enable 'Retry on Fail' and set it to 2 or 3 attempts with a 5-second interval.
Check the VPC Connector for throughput limits and adjust if necessary.
Increase the Timeout parameter under the node's Options to a higher value (e.g., 60,000ms).
agent_instruction: Enable 'Retry on Fail' in the HTTP Request node settings and increase the Timeout parameter.
